hard_invariants: GPT-5.5/GPT advisory output is high-reasoning machine output, never human/editor approval.
No human-in-the-loop is introduced as a normal/required production dependency; routine uncertainty routes through automated dispositions.
Weak/local fallback is forbidden for safety-critical editorial reasoning.
No source/claim/editorial DB mutation unless an explicit future machine gate permits it.
No docs/book mutation unless an explicit future publication/chapter gate permits it.
No claim insertion unless an explicit future claim-insertion gate permits it.
Blocked editorial state dominates all chapter mutation paths.

author_allowed, publication_approved, eligible_for_claim_insertion, eligible_for_authoring, eligible_for_publication, and chapter_update_allowed remain false unless an explicit future promotion gate changes them.

no_human_in_loop_policy.normal_routing: automated dispositions such as caveat_only, safe_reports_only, needs_more_sources, needs_better_authoring_metadata, source_context_unclear, exclude_from_pipeline, contradiction_review_required, blocked_for_publication_by_policy

open_risks: Config update proposed by Run 32 is not yet applied; current config lacks authoring-metadata promotion states/dispositions/transitions.
Runs 15–32 artifacts appear uncommitted before this handoff unless the final checkpoint commit includes them.
Pipeline has many bespoke scripts and needs consolidation before unattended production.
Verification commands regenerate protected generated artifacts that must be restored.
Downstream prose-promotion risk remains if later gates strip caveats or overclaim Hermes/OpenClaw relationship.

pipeline_architecture.summary: Inputs remain source and capture artifacts; raw captures must not flow directly to prose.
Source-card draft/report stages summarize possible source material without publication approval.
Semantic object drafts extract bounded entities/relations from source material.
GPT-5.5 and deterministic checks review whether source material supports bounded claims and whether corroboration is needed.
Run 16 persisted one source_notes row after a controlled review path; persistence still did not imply authoring or publication.
Run 17 created report-only eligibility routing from persisted review notes.
Run 18 grouped research objects into candidate clusters.
Run 19 reviewed cluster quality and retained caveat-only constraints.
Run 20 built report-only packet candidates, not prose.
Run 21 red-teamed packet candidates and routed only caveat-only author-input readiness.
Run 22A introduced closed-loop state-machine vocabulary and hard invariants.
Run 22B built metadata-only draft input candidates.
Run 23 GPT-5.5 preflighted draft-input readiness for a controlled canary.
Run 24 generated report-only canary text, not chapter prose.
Run 25 found the first canary safe but not useful.
Run 26 rebuilt/enriched author-draft input rather than expanding prose.
Run 27 preflighted rebuilt input for a second controlled canary.
Run 28 generated a second constrained report-only caveat-only canary.
Run 29 judged canary v2 safe and improved but still thin.
Run 30 packaged the canary and provenance into deterministic metadata only.
Run 31 GPT-5.5 judged metadata safe/useful as metadata only for promotion-contract work.
Run 32 created a report-only plan showing config update is needed; config was not changed.

production_readiness_gaps: Run cadence is too slow for production unattended operation.
Too many bespoke scripts per state transition; needs consolidation.
Full pytest/MkDocs on every tiny metadata step is heavy; need reduced vs full verification policy.
Verification dirties generated protected artifacts and then restores them.
Need reusable closed-loop run summary/report writer.
Need promotion-contract engine instead of hand-coded per-run checks.
Need observability and audit trail for unattended operation.
Need bounded retries and model/provider health rules.
Need automated dispositions everywhere instead of human stops.
